  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Project Controls Manager",
  "description": "Tokiye Integrated Medical Service was created to address the critical needs in healthcare project management with an emphasis on effective and efficient service delivery. The company is driven by a passion for excellence and a commitment to professional service, aiming to elevate Nigeria&rsquo;s healthcare system to international standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Project Controls Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Uyo, Akwa Ibom
Job Type: Full time

Job Description


	The Project Controls Manager will serve as the custodian of schedule, cost, progress measurement, and performance reporting for the delivery of an international-standard healthcare facility of national significance.
	This role is responsible for establishing and maintaining robust project control systems that ensure transparency, predictability, and disciplined execution across the project lifecycle.
	The Project Controls Manager will provide accurate, data-driven insights to support executive decision-making, prevent schedule and cost overruns, and enable successful project delivery.


Key Responsibilities
Project Controls Leadership and Governance:


	Establish, implement, and manage the Project Controls Framework, covering planning, scheduling, cost control, risk management, and performance reporting.
	Develop and maintain project controls policies, procedures, and standards aligned with international best practices.
	Define baseline scope, schedule, and cost parameters and ensure strict change control governance.
	Serve as the single source of truth for project performance data.


Planning, Scheduling and Progress Control:


	Develop and maintain the Integrated Master Schedule (IMS) using Primavera P6 and/or MS Project.
	Ensure full integration of design, procurement, construction, testing, and commissioning schedules.
	Establish schedule baselines, milestones, and critical path analyses.
	Monitor schedule performance, identify slippages, and recommend recovery and acceleration strategies.
	Validate contractors&rsquo; schedules and progress updates for accuracy and realism.
	Implement earned value management (EVM) techniques to track schedule performance objectively.


Cost Control, Forecasting and Performance Management:


	Establish and maintain the project cost control system, ensuring accurate tracking of budgets, commitments, actual costs, and forecasts.
	Monitor cost performance against approved budgets and forecasts.
	Prepare detailed variance analyses and identify trends that may impact project financial outcomes.
	Support cash flow forecasting and funding alignment in collaboration with Finance and Commercial teams.
	Review contractors&rsquo; cost reports, payment applications, and change cost proposals from a controls perspective.


Change Management and Risk Control:


	Manage the change control process, ensuring all variations are properly evaluated for schedule and cost impacts before approval.
	Maintain a comprehensive project risk and opportunity register.
	Quantify risk impacts and support mitigation and contingency planning.
	Provide early warnings to management on emerging risks and potential deviations from baseline plans.


Reporting, Dashboards and Executive Communication:


	Prepare and issue weekly, monthly, and executive-level project controls reports.
	Develop performance dashboards covering schedule, cost, risk, procurement, and progress KPIs.
	Present clear, concise insights to executive leadership, highlighting risks, trends, and required decisions.
	Ensure reporting accuracy, consistency, and audit-ready documentation always.


Interface with Project, Commercial and Construction Teams:


	Work closely with the Project Manager, Design Manager, Commercial Manager, and Construction teams to ensure alignment between plans and execution.
	Support commercial and contract teams in evaluating claims, extensions of time, and variations.
	Validate progress measurements and earned value claims from contractors.
	Ensure procurement and long-lead items are fully integrated into the master schedule.


Systems, Data Integrity and Digital Controls:


	Implement and manage project controls software and digital tools, including Primavera P6, ERP systems, Power Bi and reporting platforms.
	Ensure high data integrity, traceability, and version control across all planning and reporting outputs.
	Support digitalization and continuous improvement of project controls processes.


Team Leadership and Capability Development:


	Lead and mentor project controls engineers, planners, and cost controllers.
	Set performance expectations and ensure high standards of professionalism and accountability.
	Build project controls capability and promote best practices across the project team.
	Support knowledge transfer and documentation of lessons learned.


Project Close-Out and Performance Evaluation:


	Support final costs and schedule close-out activities.
	Ensure final project performance metrics, reports, and records are completed and archived.
	Participate in post-project reviews and performance evaluations.
	Capture and document lessons learned for future projects.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Engineering, Construction Management, Quantity Surveying, or related discipline.
	Master&rsquo;s Degree or professional certification (PMP, AACE, PRINCE2) is an advantage.
	Must have 12&ndash;15 years of experience in project controls on large-scale construction or infrastructure projects.
	Proven experience managing schedules and cost controls for multi-million-dollar projects.
	Strong hands-on expertise in Primavera P6, MS Project, and project reporting systems.
	Experience in hospital or complex institutional projects is highly desirable.


Skills and Competencies:


	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Excellent attention to detail and data accuracy.
	Ability to communicate complex performance data clearly to senior stakeholders.
	Strong commercial and contract awareness.
	Proactive mindset with strong risk identification capability.
	High integrity, objectivity, and professional discipline.


Benefits


	This role offers a unique opportunity for an experienced Nigerian or international project controls professional to play a pivotal role in delivering one of Nigeria&rsquo;s most prestigious healthcare infrastructure projects.&nbsp;
	You will operate at the heart of executive decision-making, providing insights that directly influence project success.
	The position offers exposure to international best practices, advanced project controls systems, and a highly governed delivery environment.
	We offer a competitive remuneration package, performance-based incentives, and long-term career growth opportunities within a rapidly expanding healthcare infrastructure organization

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Construction Manager",
  "description": "Tokiye Integrated Medical Service was created to address the critical needs in healthcare project management with an emphasis on effective and efficient service delivery. The company is driven by a passion for excellence and a commitment to professional service, aiming to elevate Nigeria&rsquo;s healthcare system to international standards.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position:&nbsp;Construction Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Uyo, Akwa Ibom
Job Type: Full time

Job Description


	The Construction Manager is responsible for the effective planning, coordination, and execution of all on-site construction activities, ensuring that the project is delivered safely, on schedule, within budget, and in full compliance with approved designs, specifications, quality standards, and regulatory requirements.&nbsp;
	The role serves as the principal site execution authority, translating project plans into structured construction activities while coordinating multiple contractors, disciplines, and stakeholders to achieve seamless delivery from groundwork through commissioning and handover.


Key Responsibilities
Construction Planning and Execution:


	Plan, organize, and manage day-to-day site construction activities across all disciplines (civil,&nbsp;structural, architectural, MEP, finishes, and specialist installations).
	Develop detailed construction execution strategies aligned with the approved Project Execution Plan and master schedule.
	Coordinate site mobilization, logistics planning, access routes, work sequencing, and temporary works.
	Ensure works are executed in strict accordance with approved drawings, technical specifications, method statements, and construction programs.
	Drive productivity and ensure construction outputs meet daily, weekly, and monthly targets.


Technical Management and Coordination:


	Interpret construction drawings, specifications, and technical documents to guide execution teams.
	Identify constructability challenges, design conflicts, and sequencing risks early and implement solutions.
	Coordinate technical interfaces between civil, structural, architectural, MEP, and specialist contractors.
	Review and approve method statements, work procedures, and inspection and test plans (ITPs).
	Liaise closely with consultants, design teams, and project engineers to resolve technical queries and RFIs.


Schedule, Cost and Resource Control:


	Take charge in monitoring and controlling construction schedules and milestone achievement.
	Track daily progress against approved work programs and implement recovery actions where slippage occurs.
	Apply critical path analysis to identify schedule risks and adjust resources accordingly.
	Monitor actual progress against planned targets and recommend recovery actions for delays.
	Liaise with Procurement, Logistics, and Engineering teams to align material and equipment delivery with planned activities.
	Identify cost drivers, inefficiencies, and potential overruns and recommend corrective measures.
	Support measurement of executed works and validation of quantities for progress reporting.


Quality Assurance and HSE Compliance:


	Ensure construction works meet approved drawings, specifications, and QA/QC standards.
	Ensure all site activities are conducted in accordance with approved risk assessments and method statements
	Enforce strict compliance with HSE policies, conducting toolbox talks and safety audits.
	Coordinate inspections, tests, mock-ups, and approvals with consultants and quality teams.
	Enforce compliance with QA/QC standards on all inspections.
	Ensure non-conformances are promptly addressed and closed.
	Drive a &ldquo;right-first-time&rdquo; culture to minimize rework and defects on construction and finishing works.


Resource and Materials Management:


	Monitor and control usage of materials, tools, and equipment to minimize waste.
	Coordinate with Procurement and Logistics to ensure timely delivery of materials.
	Maintain an accurate inventory of site materials and assets.
	Coordinate timely delivery and efficient use of materials, equipment, and manpower.
	Implement contingency measures in case of delays, weather disruptions, or design variations.


Contractor and Subcontractor Management:


	Manage and coordinate subcontractors to ensure adherence to scope, schedule, quality, and safety&nbsp;requirements.
	Chair daily coordination meetings, and weekly site progress meetings.
	Monitor subcontractor performance and enforce corrective actions where required.
	Ensure subcontractors maintain adequate manpower, equipment, and supervision on site.


Stakeholder Communication and Reporting:


	Serve as the primary on-site coordination point between the Project Director, site teams,&nbsp;subcontractors, and consultants.
	Maintain good relationships with consultants, vendors, and community stakeholders.
	Handle conflicts and grievances professionally to ensure site harmony.
	Provide clear, accurate daily, weekly and monthly progress updates to project leadership.
	Escalate risks, delays, and technical challenges promptly with proposed solutions.


Documentation and Records Management:


	Ensure accurate daily site records, including manpower logs, equipment usage, weather reports,&nbsp;and progress diaries.
	Maintain records of inspections, approvals, and as-built information.
	Support proper documentation for handover, commissioning, and close-out activities.


Team Leadership and Development:


	Lead, mentor, and supervise site engineers, supervisors, foremen, and construction staff.
	Set clear performance expectations and promote accountability on site.
	Foster teamwork, discipline, and professional conduct across the workforce.
	Support skills development and knowledge transfer within the site team.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or related discipline.
	Minimum of 15 years of experience in construction execution roles, with at least 5 years as a Construction Manager or Senior Site Manager.
	Proven experience delivering large-scale projects such as hospitals, high-rise buildings, or major infrastructure works.
	Strong understanding of construction methodologies, sequencing, and site logistics.
	Experience managing multiple subcontractors and large multidisciplinary site teams.


Skills and Competencies:


	Strong leadership and on-site decision-making ability.
	Excellent technical knowledge of construction works and drawings.
	High attention to detail with strong quality orientation.
	Ability to manage complex site operations under schedule pressure.
	Strong communication, coordination, and reporting skills.
	High integrity, accountability, and safety-driven mindset.


Benefits


	This role offers the opportunity to play a key leadership role in the delivery of a landmark construction project executed to international standards.
	The Construction Manager will operate in a structured, professionally governed project environment, with clear authority, strong support systems, and the opportunity to leave a lasting impact on a high-profile development.

Job Description

	The Construction Manager is responsible for the effective planning, coordination, and execution of all on-site construction activities, ensuring that the project is delivered safely, on schedule, within budget, and in full compliance with approved designs, specifications, quality standards, and regulatory requirements. 
	The role serves as the principal site execution authority, translating project plans into structured construction activities while coordinating multiple contractors, disciplines, and stakeholders to achieve seamless delivery from groundwork through commissioning and handover.


Key Responsibilities
Construction Planning and Execution:


	Plan, organize, and manage day-to-day site construction activities across all disciplines (civil, structural, architectural, MEP, finishes, and specialist installations).
	Develop detailed construction execution strategies aligned with the approved Project Execution Plan and master schedule.
	Coordinate site mobilization, logistics planning, access routes, work sequencing, and temporary works.
	Ensure works are executed in strict accordance with approved drawings, technical specifications, method statements, and construction programs.
	Drive productivity and ensure construction outputs meet daily, weekly, and monthly targets.


Technical Management and Coordination:


	Interpret construction drawings, specifications, and technical documents to guide execution teams.
	Identify constructability challenges, design conflicts, and sequencing risks early and implement solutions.
	Coordinate technical interfaces between civil, structural, architectural, MEP, and specialist contractors.
	Review and approve method statements, work procedures, and inspection and test plans (ITPs).
	Liaise closely with consultants, design teams, and project engineers to resolve technical queries and RFIs.


Schedule, Cost and Resource Control:


	Take charge in monitoring and controlling construction schedules and milestone achievement.
	Track daily progress against approved work programs and implement recovery actions where slippage occurs.
	Apply critical path analysis to identify schedule risks and adjust resources accordingly.
	Monitor actual progress against planned targets and recommend recovery actions for delays.
	Liaise with Procurement, Logistics, and Engineering teams to align material and equipment delivery with planned activities.
	Identify cost drivers, inefficiencies, and potential overruns and recommend corrective measures.
	Support measurement of executed works and validation of quantities for progress reporting.


Quality Assurance and HSE Compliance:


	Ensure construction works meet approved drawings, specifications, and QA/QC standards.
	Ensure all site activities are conducted in accordance with approved risk assessments and method statements
	Enforce strict compliance with HSE policies, conducting toolbox talks and safety audits.
	Coordinate inspections, tests, mock-ups, and approvals with consultants and quality teams.
	Enforce compliance with QA/QC standards on all inspections.
	Ensure non-conformances are promptly addressed and closed.
	Drive a “right-first-time” culture to minimize rework and defects on construction and finishing works.


Resource and Materials Management:


	Monitor and control usage of materials, tools, and equipment to minimize waste.
	Coordinate with Procurement and Logistics to ensure timely delivery of materials.
	Maintain an accurate inventory of site materials and assets.
	Coordinate timely delivery and efficient use of materials, equipment, and manpower.
	Implement contingency measures in case of delays, weather disruptions, or design variations.


Contractor and Subcontractor Management:


	Manage and coordinate subcontractors to ensure adherence to scope, schedule, quality, and safety requirements.
	Chair daily coordination meetings, and weekly site progress meetings.
	Monitor subcontractor performance and enforce corrective actions where required.
	Ensure subcontractors maintain adequate manpower, equipment, and supervision on site.


Stakeholder Communication and Reporting:


	Serve as the primary on-site coordination point between the Project Director, site teams, subcontractors, and consultants.
	Maintain good relationships with consultants, vendors, and community stakeholders.
	Handle conflicts and grievances professionally to ensure site harmony.
	Provide clear, accurate daily, weekly and monthly progress updates to project leadership.
	Escalate risks, delays, and technical challenges promptly with proposed solutions.


Documentation and Records Management:


	Ensure accurate daily site records, including manpower logs, equipment usage, weather reports, and progress diaries.
	Maintain records of inspections, approvals, and as-built information.
	Support proper documentation for handover, commissioning, and close-out activities.


Team Leadership and Development:


	Lead, mentor, and supervise site engineers, supervisors, foremen, and construction staff.
	Set clear performance expectations and promote accountability on site.
	Foster teamwork, discipline, and professional conduct across the workforce.
	Support skills development and knowledge transfer within the site team.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Management, or related discipline.
	Minimum of 15 years of experience in construction execution roles, with at least 5 years as a Construction Manager or Senior Site Manager.
	Proven experience delivering large-scale projects such as hospitals, high-rise buildings, or major infrastructure works.
	Strong understanding of construction methodologies, sequencing, and site logistics.
	Experience managing multiple subcontractors and large multidisciplinary site teams.


Skills and Competencies:


	Strong leadership and on-site decision-making ability.
	Excellent technical knowledge of construction works and drawings.
	High attention to detail with strong quality orientation.
	Ability to manage complex site operations under schedule pressure.
	Strong communication, coordination, and reporting skills.
	High integrity, accountability, and safety-driven mindset.


Benefits


	This role offers the opportunity to play a key leadership role in the delivery of a landmark construction project executed to international standards.
	The Construction Manager will operate in a structured, professionally governed project environment, with clear authority, strong support systems, and the opportunity to leave a lasting impact on a high-profile development.

Executive & Operations Assistant Job at Work in Nigeria




        
    

        
        Work in Nigeria is a talent recruitment and headhunting company based in Lagos Nigeria. Our overall goal is to redefine the recruitment sector by sourcing, shortlisting and placing suitable and top-notch talent in for our client’s businesses. We provide top notch and streamlined recruitment and candidate placement services on a permanent, contractual and interim basis across all grade levels and sectors.

At Work In Nigeria, we also provide employability and career advisory support to individuals and career professionals who require expert coaching and guidance with making career decisions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive & Operations Assistant
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Core Responsibilities
Executive Administration & Schedule Management:


	Proactively manage complex personal and business calendars, ensuring no conflicts between corporate deadlines and personal commitments.
	Act as the primary gatekeeper for emails and official correspondence, crafting professional responses that align with the brands' sophisticated tone.
	Coordinate virtual meetings and provide on-call tech support to ensure seamless collaboration.


Marketing & Brand Coordination:


	Serve as the Project Coordinator for Alexandrite and Isibhaho’me marketing campaigns, tracking deadlines from concept to execution.
	Execute social media management: oversee content scheduling, community engagement, and basic content creation across all brand and personal accounts.
	Develop high-end presentations and reports using Canva, Google Slides, or PowerPoint for stakeholders and clients.


Financial & Data Integrity:


	Maintain meticulous records through data entry, bookkeeping, and expense tracking (experience with QuickBooks preferred).
	Conduct deep-dive market research and competitor analysis to keep the luxury brands at the forefront of the industry.


Lifestyle & Business Logistics:


	Manage business and personal errands, including travel arrangements and vendor follow-ups.
	Anticipate needs by managing "floating" reminders and ensuring all long-term projects stay on track.


Key Competencies


	Refined Communication: Ability to mirror a luxury brand voice in all written and verbal interactions.
	Problem-Solving: A proactive "find a way" attitude toward logistical or technical hurdles.
	Discretion: Handling sensitive business data and personal schedules with the utmost confidentiality.


Salary
N250,000 per month.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.
